mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131516.0 thousand persons indicates ongoing employment growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth rate and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or monthly PAYEMS increases fail to precede positive SPX returns within 20 trading days in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, monetary tightening constrains credit availability and raises discount rates on future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism activates only when FEDFUNDS crosses above this threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS currently at 0.07% indicates accommodative policy
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns is positive (> 0.05) during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, equity valuations expand due to improved earnings visibility and economic momentum. This interaction effect is stronger than either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 397666.0 million dollars and HOUST at 658.0 thousand units
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (RSAFS growth × HOUST growth) with 20-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint positive signals in both series fail to precede positive SPX returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 94.0 signal supply-side weakness and margin compression across the economy. This triggers negative equity returns as investors reprice earnings expectations downward within a 5-trading-day window.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.1851 is near the critical threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of instances when INDPRO < 94.0, or Rank IC between INDPRO level and forward returns is positive during supply weakness periods.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 8.5% signal labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. This regime shift triggers negative equity returns as investors anticipate lower corporate revenues and earnings. The mechanism is regime-conditional and reverses direction when UNRATE falls below 6.0%.
regime_hint: labor market weakness or recession risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE > 8.5%, or Rank IC between UNRATE and forward returns is negative (< -0.05) during periods of low unemployment (UNRATE < 5.0%).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL and monetary policy stance (FEDFUNDS) interact such that when inflation exceeds 3.0% while FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0%, real rates are negative and equity valuations compress. This joint condition triggers negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 226.955 and FEDFUNDS at 0.07% indicate potential real rate compression
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS inverse) with 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive returns in > 60% of instances when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter signals underlying economic momentum. Positive GDP growth acceleration produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon as investors reprice growth expectations upward. This mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or quarters with positive GDP growth fail to produce positive SPX returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure supply-side capacity and labor utilization. When both decline simultaneously, it signals stagflation risk and triggers negative equity returns within 20 trading days as investors flee growth assets.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline AND PAYEMS month-over-month decline
evidence_refs: fact:Both series at current levels require monitoring for simultaneous declines
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint decline signal with 20-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive returns in > 55% of instances when both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline month-over-month.
